board.title: Human-Computer Interaction
board.author: Julius Boateng
board.description: Human-Computer Interaction was popularized by the 1983 book "The Psychology of Human-Computer Interaction." HCI is now an established field at the intersection of CS, behavioral science, and design. This puzzle explores key HCI concepts and people.

clues.question: Design principle where essential functions and system state are readily visible to users
Predictive model where a user's decision time increases logarithmically with the number of choices; applies to selecting from a menu
Elements that restrict a user's possible actions; include physical, semantic, and logical elements
Usability researcher who developed the "10 Usability Heuristics for User Interface Design"; emphasized interfaces should match users' prior experience
Cues that indicate a user's possible actions and how to perform them
Design concept where users readily perceive possible actions based on context and prior experience
System response that informs users of actions, results, and state; reduces the gulf of evaluation
Researcher at Xerox PARC who co-authored "The Psychology of Human-Computer Interaction" and developed models like GOMS and the Model Human Processor
Relationship between controls and their effects
Delay between a user's action and system response
Cognitive scientist who formalized user-centered design in "User-Centered System Design" and popularized concepts like affordances and signifiers
Computer scientist who defined direct manipulation interfaces and developed the "Eight Golden Rules of Interface Design"
Current condition of a system that determines its behavior and possible interactions
Predictive model where the time to reach a target depends on distance and size; applies to pointing tasks
